_comment: Maps each gold-set CSV to its tender PDF + the page range the gold covers. Add an entry when you finish labelling a tender (see labelling-guide.md). Set "draft": true for in-progress / example-only gold so the aggregate eval skips it. Run: python -m engine.scripts.eval_all

tenders.title: MAC Museum Cleaning Services ITT
tenders.note: REJECTED — MIS-SOURCED, not merely mis-formatted (verified 2026-07-01). The PDF data/tenders/museum-cleaning-itt.pdf is byte-identical (md5 52f03df8...) to data/tenders/Cleaning-ITT-Version-1.3-FINAL-1.pdf — the MAC (Metropolitan Arts Centre), Belfast cleaning ITT — yet these 92 labels contain SPSO-2013 content (the Ombudsman, s.19 SPSO Act 2002, eblows@spso.org.uk, deadline 06/11/2013). i.e. SPSO labels bolted onto the wrong PDF. This CANNOT be fixed by cleaning gating/type/pages; it needs a full re-label from the real MAC PDF per labelling-guide.md, or the entry should be dropped. (Also mis-formatted: 88/92 is_gating=true; type uses Specification/Commercial not the mandatory/optional enum; source_page is strings.) Stays draft:true — excluded from the scored aggregate. The demo's accuracy claim is therefore ONE validated tender (SPSO); 'across N tenders' is robustness, not recall. See comms G-014 / B-002.
